questions.text: What is the primary message conveyed in the opening verses of Surah Hud?
questions.answer: The perfection of the Quranic verses and the command to worship none but Allah.

questions.text: What should a person do to receive abundant provision and a good life until a decreed term?
questions.answer: Seek forgiveness from Allah and turn to Him in repentance.

questions.text: How does Allah describe His knowledge of the secret thoughts and actions of people?
questions.answer: He knows what they conceal and what they declare; He is Knowing of that within the breasts.

questions.text: How does Allah describe the provision of every living creature on earth?
questions.answer: Allah has guaranteed the provision (Rizq) for every creature and knows its dwelling and its repository.

questions.text: Where was Allah's Throne (Arsh) before the creation of the heavens and the earth?
questions.answer: It was upon the water.

questions.text: What is the primary purpose of the creation of the heavens and the earth as mentioned in this Surah?
questions.answer: To test mankind as to which of them is best in deed.

questions.text: How does man react when Allah gives him a taste of mercy and then takes it away?
questions.answer: He becomes despairing and ungrateful.

questions.text: How does man react when Allah gives him favor after hardship has touched him?
questions.answer: He says, 'The bad times have gone from me,' and becomes exultant and boastful.

questions.text: Who are the exceptions to the typical human reaction of despair and pride?
questions.answer: Those who are patient and do righteous deeds; they will have forgiveness and a great reward.

questions.text: What was the challenge posed to the disbelievers regarding the Quran in this Surah?
questions.answer: They were challenged to bring ten invented surahs like it if they were truthful.

questions.text: What is the fate of those who desire only the life of this world and its adornment?
questions.answer: Allah will repay them in full for their deeds in this world, but in the Hereafter, they will have nothing but the Fire.

questions.text: What is the parable given for the two groups (believers and disbelievers)?
questions.answer: One is like the blind and deaf, and the other is like the seeing and hearing. Are they equal in comparison?

questions.text: What was the specific reply of Nuh (AS) when his people accused him of being a mere human like them?
questions.answer: He clarified that he does not possess the treasures of Allah, nor does he know the unseen, but follows clear proof from his Lord.

questions.text: What did the chiefs of Nuh's people say about those who followed him?
questions.answer: They said they were only the lowest of them who followed him without thought.

questions.text: Did Nuh (AS) ask for any payment for his preaching?
questions.answer: No, he said his reward is only from Allah.

questions.text: What did Nuh's people do when he was building the Ark?
questions.answer: Whenever the chiefs of his people passed by him, they mocked him.

questions.text: What was the sign that the punishment was about to start for Nuh's people?
questions.answer: When the 'oven' (Tannur) overflowed with water.

questions.text: Who and what was Nuh (AS) commanded to carry in the Ark?
questions.answer: Two of every kind (male and female), his family (except those against whom the word had preceded), and those who believed.

questions.text: What was the prayer Nuh (AS) said when the Ark began to move?
questions.answer: 'In the name of Allah is its course and its anchorage. Indeed, my Lord is Forgiving and Merciful.'

questions.text: What happened to the son of Nuh (AS) during the flood?
questions.answer: He refused to board the ark, seeking refuge on a mountain, and was drowned among the disbelievers.

questions.text: What was the 'mountain' Nuh's son intended to climb, and what did Nuh (AS) tell him?
questions.answer: The son thought a mountain would protect him from the water, but Nuh said, 'There is no protector today from the decree of Allah, except for whom He has mercy.'

questions.text: How did Nuh (AS) respond when Allah told him his son was 'not of his family'?
questions.answer: He realized that family ties are spiritual, based on righteous deeds and faith, rather than just blood; he then sought Allah's forgiveness for asking about what he did not know.

questions.text: Where did the Ark of Nuh (AS) eventually come to rest?
questions.answer: On Mount Judi.

questions.text: What was the central call of Prophet Hud to the people of 'Ad?
questions.answer: To worship Allah alone and seek His forgiveness so He would send abundant rain and increase their strength.

questions.text: What did the people of 'Ad say about their idols in relation to Hud (AS)?
questions.answer: They claimed that some of their gods had possessed him with evil (insanity).

questions.text: How were the people of 'Ad destroyed?
questions.answer: By a screaming, violent wind that left them lying like uprooted trunks of palm trees.

questions.text: Which Prophet was sent to the people of Thamud?
questions.answer: Prophet Salih.

questions.text: What did Salih (AS) tell his people about their origin from the earth?
questions.answer: He said that Allah produced them from the earth and settled them in it.

questions.text: What was the miraculous sign given to the people of Thamud?
questions.answer: A She-Camel which was a sign from Allah; they were commanded to let her eat on Allah's earth and not touch her with harm.

questions.text: What happened after the people of Thamud hamstrung the She-Camel?
questions.answer: Salih told them to enjoy themselves in their homes for three days, after which the punishment (a shriek/earthquake) destroyed them.

questions.text: Why did Ibrahim (AS) feel fear when the angels visited him?
questions.answer: Because they did not reach out to eat the roasted calf he had prepared for them.

questions.text: What glad tidings did the angels give to Ibrahim (AS) and his wife?
questions.answer: The birth of Ishaq (Isaac), and after Ishaq, Ya'qub (Jacob).

questions.text: What was the reaction of Ibrahim's wife to the news of a child?
questions.answer: She laughed and said, 'Woe to me! Shall I give birth while I am an old woman and this, my husband, is an old man?'

questions.text: How did the angels respond to Ibrahim's wife's amazement?
questions.answer: They asked, 'Are you amazed at the decree of Allah? May the mercy of Allah and His blessings be upon you, people of the house.'

questions.text: Why did Ibrahim (AS) try to argue/plead with the angels regarding the people of Lut?
questions.answer: Because he was forbearing, compassionate, and constantly turning to Allah (Munib).

questions.text: How did Prophet Lut (AS) feel when the angels arrived at his home?
questions.answer: He felt distressed and straitened for them because he knew the evil nature of his people.

questions.text: What did the people of Lut demand when they came rushing to his house?
questions.answer: They demanded his guests for their immoral purposes.

questions.text: What did Lut (AS) offer his people to protect his guests?
questions.answer: He told them to marry his daughters (the women of the community) as they were purer for them, and to fear Allah.

questions.text: What was the specific punishment for the people of Lut?
questions.answer: Their city was turned upside down and stones of baked clay, marked from their Lord, were rained upon them.

questions.text: Which Prophet was sent to the people of Madyan?
questions.answer: Prophet Shu'ayb.

questions.text: What economic crimes did Shu'ayb (AS) warn his people against?
questions.answer: Cheating in weights and measures and spreading corruption on earth.

questions.text: What is the 'Baqiyyatullah' mentioned by Prophet Shu'ayb?
questions.answer: That which remains with Allah (lawful profit or the reward for obedience) is better for the believers.

questions.text: How did the people of Madyan mock Shu'ayb's prayer (Salat)?
questions.answer: They asked if his prayer commanded him that they should leave what their fathers worshipped or stop doing what they willed with their own wealth.

questions.text: What was the punishment for the people of Madyan?
questions.answer: A shriek (Sarihah) seized them, and they became corpses in their homes.

questions.text: How does Allah describe the fate of Pharaoh and his followers on the Day of Resurrection?
questions.answer: Pharaoh will lead his people on the Day of Resurrection and lead them into the Fire.

questions.text: Were the destroyed nations wronged by Allah?
questions.answer: No, 'We did not wrong them, but they wronged themselves.'

questions.text: What will be the state of the 'wretched' on the Day of Judgment?
questions.answer: They will be in the Fire, where there will be for them [violent] exhaling and inhaling.

questions.text: What will be the reward for those who are 'blessed'?
questions.answer: They will be in Paradise, abiding therein as long as the heavens and the earth endure, unless Allah wills otherwise—a gift without end.

questions.text: What is the command given to the Prophet (SAW) and those who have turned to Allah regarding their conduct?
questions.answer: To stand firm (Istiqamah) as they have been commanded and not to transgress.

questions.text: What is the warning regarding 'leaning' toward the wrongdoers?
questions.answer: Believers are told not to lean toward those who do wrong, lest the Fire touch them, and they would have no protectors besides Allah.

questions.text: When are the times for establishing prayer mentioned in this Surah?
questions.answer: At the two ends of the day and at the approach of the night.

questions.text: What effect do good deeds have on evil deeds?
questions.answer: Good deeds remove evil deeds; this is a reminder for those who remember.

questions.text: Why did Allah not destroy the towns while their people were reformers (Muslihun)?
questions.answer: Because Allah is never unjust to destroy a population while they are actively doing good.

questions.text: What is the purpose of the stories of the Prophets mentioned in this Surah?
questions.answer: To make the heart of the Prophet (SAW) firm and to provide truth, an admonition, and a reminder for the believers.

questions.text: What is the final instruction in the Surah regarding trust in Allah?
questions.answer: To worship Him and rely upon Him (Tawakkul), for He is not unaware of what you do.

questions.text: How many years did the Ark take to build (implied by the mockery)?
questions.answer: The Surah doesn't specify the years, but it emphasizes that the mockery was continuous throughout the building process.

questions.text: What did Hud (AS) say about his own power versus Allah's power?
questions.answer: He said that he relies on Allah, his Lord and their Lord; there is no creature but that He holds it by its forelock (complete control).

questions.text: What was the 'word of your Lord' that was fulfilled regarding Hell?
questions.answer: That He will surely fill Hell with Jinn and men all together.

questions.text: What is the 'clear authority' (Sultanum-mubin) given to Musa?
questions.answer: The miracles and proofs that established his prophethood.

questions.text: What is the 'repayment in full' for deeds in this world?
questions.answer: Receiving the worldly benefits of one's efforts without any reduction, but having no share in the Hereafter.

questions.text: How does the Surah describe those who invent lies about Allah?
questions.answer: They will be presented before their Lord, and the witnesses will say, 'These are the ones who lied against their Lord.' The curse of Allah is upon the wrongdoers.

questions.text: What is the 'double punishment' mentioned for those who avert others from the way of Allah?
questions.answer: The punishment will be multiplied for them because they could not hear or see the truth.

questions.text: What is the 'noble provision' (Rizqun Karim)?
questions.answer: The eternal reward in Paradise.

questions.text: What did the people of Nuh say about his followers being 'simple-minded'?
questions.answer: They said they followed him without mature reflection (Badiya al-rayi).

questions.text: How did Nuh (AS) describe his mission in terms of wealth?
questions.answer: He said, 'I do not ask you for wealth for it; my reward is only from Allah.'

questions.text: What is the 'mercy' that Nuh (AS) said his Lord had given him?
questions.answer: His prophethood and the clear proof of faith.

questions.text: What is the 'shameful punishment'?
questions.answer: The punishment in this life and the next for those who mock the truth.

questions.text: What is the 'eternal punishment'?
questions.answer: The punishment of Hell which will never end.

questions.text: What did Allah command the earth and the sky to do after the flood?
questions.answer: 'O earth, swallow your water, and O sky, withhold [your rain].' And the water subsided.

questions.text: What is the 'peace' with which Nuh (AS) was told to disembark?
questions.answer: A blessing from Allah upon him and upon the nations that would follow him.

questions.text: What is the 'increase in strength' promised by Hud (AS)?
questions.answer: Physical and material power granted to those who seek forgiveness.

questions.text: What is the 'evidence' (Bayyinah) that Salih (AS) brought?
questions.answer: The miraculous she-camel.

questions.text: What is the 'roasted calf' a sign of?
questions.answer: Ibrahim's hospitality and generosity.

questions.text: What is the 'unavoidable decree' regarding the people of Lut?
questions.answer: The punishment that could not be repelled because of their persistent sin.

questions.text: What is the 'reform' (Islah) that Shu'ayb (AS) desired?
questions.answer: Improving the spiritual and social condition of his people as much as he was able.

questions.text: What is the 'success' (Tawfiq) of Shu'ayb?
questions.answer: It is only from Allah; on Him he relies and to Him he turns.

questions.text: What is the 'cursed' place where Pharaoh leads his people?
questions.answer: The Fire of Hell.

questions.text: What are the 'signs' of Allah that should make a person firm?
questions.answer: The stories of the prophets and the observations of nature.

questions.text: What is the 'reminder' for those who remember?
questions.answer: The Quran and the lessons from history.

questions.text: What is the 'Unseen of the heavens and the earth'?
questions.answer: Everything that is hidden from human perception, known only to Allah.
